evals.prompt: Hey, I need to create a BlogPage content type for our company blog. It should have a title (required), an author name, publish date, featured image, article body (needs to support rich formatting), and a list of tags. Also we want to be able to add related articles at the bottom. Our content lives in src/components and we already have optimizely.config.mjs set up. Can you help me model this?
evals.expected_output: Should create BlogPage.tsx in src/components directory with contentType() definition including: title (string, required), author (string), publishDate (dateTime), featuredImage (content or contentReference with allowedTypes: ['_image']), body (richText), tags (array with items: {type: 'string'}), relatedArticles (array of content references to other BlogPage or Article types). Should use baseType: '_page'. Should find and update initContentTypeRegistry if it exists. Should remind user to run 'npx @optimizely/cms-cli@latest config push optimizely.config.mjs' and offer to run it.

assertions.name: Creates TypeScript file with .tsx or .ts extension
assertions.check: Output includes a file named BlogPage.tsx or BlogPage.ts

assertions.name: Imports contentType from @optimizely/cms-sdk
assertions.check: File contains: import { contentType } from '@optimizely/cms-sdk' or similar

assertions.name: Includes title property with string type
assertions.check: Properties include title field with type: 'string'

assertions.name: Includes tags as array of strings
assertions.check: Properties include tags with type: 'array' and items: { type: 'string' }

assertions.name: Includes featuredImage with image restriction
assertions.check: Properties include featuredImage with allowedTypes containing '_image'

assertions.name: Includes relatedArticles as content array
assertions.check: Properties include relatedArticles with type: 'array' of content references

evals.prompt: i'm working on a headless cms project (next.js 14 app router) and my content team wants different visual styles for how articles display - like a card view vs a list view vs a featured hero layout. i think these are called display templates? we have an Article content type already defined. how do i set up a card display template that lets editors choose whether to show the image or not?
evals.expected_output: Should create a display template file (e.g., ArticleCardTemplate.tsx) with displayTemplate() definition including: key, displayName, contentType: 'Article', settings object with a showImage setting (editor: 'checkbox', choices for true/false with displayName and sortOrder). Should find and update initDisplayTemplateRegistry if it exists. Should remind user to run config push and offer to run it. Should explain that this provides visual variation options for the Article content type.

evals.prompt: Our marketing team keeps asking me to add the same SEO fields (meta title, meta description, og:image) to every single page type we create. It's getting repetitive and I keep forgetting fields. Is there a way to define these once and reuse them across multiple content types? I saw something about contracts in the docs but I'm not sure how to set that up. We're using TypeScript and the fields should go in a group called 'seo'.
evals.expected_output: Should create an SEO contract file (e.g., SEOContract.tsx) with contract() definition including: key: 'seo', displayName: 'SEO Properties', properties including metaTitle (string with maxLength: 60), metaDescription (string with maxLength: 160), ogImage (contentReference with allowedTypes: ['_image']). Each property should have group: 'seo'. Should explain how to use the contract with extends in content types. Should find and update initContentTypeRegistry to add the contract. Should remind about config push.

evals.prompt: We're migrating from our old CMS to Optimizely and I have JSON exports of our existing content types from the CMS. Here's one for our ProductPage - can you convert this to the TypeScript format? {"contentType":[{"key":"ProductPage","displayName":"Product Page","baseType":"_page","properties":[{"name":"productName","type":"string","displayName":"Product Name"},{"name":"price","type":"float","displayName":"Price"},{"name":"description","type":"richText","displayName":"Product Description"},{"name":"images","type":"array","items":{"type":"contentReference","allowedTypes":["_image"]},"displayName":"Product Images"}]}]}
evals.expected_output: Should parse the JSON structure and create ProductPage.tsx with contentType() definition matching the JSON schema: key: 'ProductPage', displayName: 'Product Page', baseType: '_page', properties with productName (string), price (float), description (richText), images (array with items contentReference allowing _image types). Should maintain all displayName values from JSON. Should handle the array of content references correctly. Should add to registry and remind about config push.

evals.prompt: I want to create a Hero component that can be dropped into content areas on any page. It needs a heading, subheading, background image, and a CTA button (which should be an embedded component with its own text and link properties). The component should be usable in both section and element composition modes. How do I model this?
evals.expected_output: Should create Hero.tsx with contentType() definition: baseType: '_component', compositionBehaviors: ['sectionEnabled', 'elementEnabled'], properties including heading (string), subheading (string), backgroundImage (contentReference with allowedTypes: ['_image']), and ctaButton (type: 'component' with contentType pointing to a Button component or inline component definition). Should explain that components with '_component' baseType can be used in content areas. Should add to registry and remind about config push.

evals.prompt: creating a LandingPage type that should only allow certain child pages underneath it - specifically it can contain PricingPage, TestimonialPage, and more LandingPages for sub-sections. also need fields for hero title, hero subtitle, and a sections content area. this is for a marketing site. components are in ./app/components/
evals.expected_output: Should read optimizely.config.mjs to confirm components path, then create LandingPage.tsx in ./app/components/ with contentType() definition: baseType: '_page', mayContainTypes: [PricingPageContentType, TestimonialPageContentType, '_self'] (or string keys if types not imported), properties including heroTitle (string), heroSubtitle (string), sections (array or contentArea type). Should explain mayContainTypes controls the allowed child pages in the content tree. Should add to registry and remind about config push.

evals.prompt: Create a page type SkillTestingPage with 2 properties: a dropdown property p_dropdownlist with options Red, Green, Blue and a select list property p_selectlist with options Large, Medium, Small
evals.expected_output: Should create SkillTestingPage.tsx with contentType() definition containing two properties. For p_dropdownlist: MUST have type: 'string', format: 'selectOne', and enum array with objects {value: 'Red', displayName: 'Red'}, {value: 'Green', displayName: 'Green'}, {value: 'Blue', displayName: 'Blue'}. For p_selectlist: MUST have type: 'array', format: 'selectMany', and items.enum array with objects {value: 'Large', displayName: 'Large'}, {value: 'Medium', displayName: 'Medium'}, {value: 'Small', displayName: 'Small'}. Should NOT create properties without format field. Should NOT create properties without enum arrays.

evals.prompt: Create a page type SkillBug5Page with a url to document property p_urlToDocument, a url to image property p_urlToImage, a short string property p_shortString, a long string property p_longString, and a guid property p_guid
evals.expected_output: Should create SkillBug5Page.tsx with contentType() definition containing five properties. For p_urlToDocument: MUST have type: 'url', format: 'DocumentUrl'. For p_urlToImage: MUST have type: 'url', format: 'ImageUrl'. For p_shortString: MUST have type: 'string', format: 'shortString'. For p_longString: MUST have type: 'string' with NO format field. For p_guid: MUST have type: 'string', format: 'guid'. Should correctly recognize format requirements from property descriptions.

evals.prompt: Create content type TestingPage with p_string property is a long string and has display name is "string display name", description: 'A sample string field', localized (culture specific): true, group: 'content', sort index: 5, indexing type: 'searchable', start with "string", min length is 1, max length is 100
evals.expected_output: Should create TestingPage.tsx with contentType() definition containing p_string property. MUST have: type: 'string', displayName: 'string display name', description: 'A sample string field', isLocalized: true, group: 'content', sortOrder: 5, indexingType: 'searchable', pattern: '^string', minLength: 1, maxLength: 100. Should NOT omit description, isLocalized, or sortOrder fields.

evals.prompt: Create content type TestingPage with p_longstring property is a long string and required
evals.expected_output: Should create TestingPage.tsx with contentType() definition containing p_longstring property. MUST have: type: 'string', isRequired: true. Should NOT use 'required' field (wrong field name). The correct field name is 'isRequired'.

evals.prompt: Create content type TestingPage, add p_float is float property and this property has display name is "float display name", group: 'content', indexing type: 'queryable', minimum: 20, maximum: 50
evals.expected_output: Should create TestingPage.tsx with contentType() definition containing p_float property. MUST have: type: 'float', displayName: 'float display name', group: 'content', indexingType: 'queryable', minimum: 20, maximum: 50. Should NOT omit minimum or maximum constraints.

evals.prompt: Create content type TestingPage, add p_list_string is a list of string property and this property has minLength is 1, maxLength is 10, each item start with "test"
evals.expected_output: Should create TestingPage.tsx with contentType() definition containing p_list_string property. MUST have: type: 'array', items with type: 'string', items.minLength: 1, items.maxLength: 10, items.pattern: '^test'. Constraints must be INSIDE the items object, not at the array property level. Should NOT omit item-level constraints.

evals.prompt: Create content type TestingPage, add p_contentReference is contentReference property and this property has display name is "content reference display name", restrictedType is RestrictContentType, allowedType is AllowedContentType
evals.expected_output: Should create TestingPage.tsx with contentType() definition containing p_contentReference property. MUST have: type: 'contentReference', displayName: 'content reference display name', allowedTypes: [AllowedContentType] (object reference, NOT string), restrictedTypes: [RestrictContentType] (object reference). Should import AllowedContentType and RestrictContentType at top of file. Should NOT use string values like ['AllowedContentType'].

evals.prompt: Create content type TestingPage, add p_contentReference is contentReference property and this property has reference type is ReferenceContentType
evals.expected_output: Should create TestingPage.tsx with contentType() definition containing p_contentReference property. MUST have: type: 'contentReference', contentType: ReferenceContentType (object reference, singular not array). Should import ReferenceContentType at top of file. Should NOT omit contentType field.

evals.prompt: Create content type TestingPage, add p_contentReference is contentReference property and this property has restrictedType is RestrictContentType, allowedType is AllowedContentType, reference type is ReferenceContentType
evals.expected_output: Should create TestingPage.tsx with contentType() definition containing p_contentReference property. Since contentType and allowedTypes/restrictedTypes are mutually exclusive, should use ONLY contentType: ReferenceContentType and omit allowedTypes/restrictedTypes. Should warn user about the conflict. Should import ReferenceContentType at top of file.
